title: rosa canina subsp. glaberrima
intro: Rosa canina subsp. glaberrima, also known as the smooth-leaved dog rose or wild rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina plant, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of vitamin C, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rosa canina subsp. glaberrima include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and menopausal symptoms. Rosa canina subsp. glaberrima can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or oil, and is also used in skincare products due to its antioxidant and anti-aging properties.
